tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: I'd like to start tracking my research on road technology and transportation innovations. As we work on various policy initiatives, I need a systematic way to keep track of what we're exploring. Could you help me set up some initial research preferences? I'm particularly interested in sustainable pavement materials, smart highway technologies, and electric vehicle infrastructure integration. Let's start with those core areas.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments:
{"text": "Research Preferences for Road Technology and Transportation Innovations:

Core Focus Areas:
1. Sustainable pavement materials - exploring eco-friendly and recycled materials for road construction and maintenance
2. Smart highway technologies - intelligent transportation systems, sensors, connected vehicle infrastructure
3. Electric vehicle infrastructure integration - charging networks, inductive charging, grid integration for transportation

Context: Working on various policy initiatives in transportation and road infrastructure."}

messages.content:
{"memory": {"id": "m2", "user": "None", "text": "Research Preferences for Road Technology and Transportation Innovations:

Core Focus Areas:
1. Sustainable pavement materials - exploring eco-friendly and recycled materials for road construction and maintenance
2. Smart highway technologies - intelligent transportation systems, sensors, connected vehicle infrastructure
3. Electric vehicle infrastructure integration - charging networks, inductive charging, grid integration for transportation

Context: Working on various policy initiatives in transportation and road infrastructure."}}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content:
I've set up your research tracking with your three core focus areas:

1. **Sustainable pavement materials** - eco-friendly and recycled materials for road construction and maintenance
2. **Smart highway technologies** - intelligent transportation systems, sensors, and connected vehicle infrastructure
3. **Electric vehicle infrastructure integration** - charging networks, inductive charging, and grid integration

Your preferences are now stored and ready to help us stay organized as you explore these policy initiatives. Let me know when you'd like to add more areas or start diving into any of these topics!

Now that we have those initial preferences stored, could you search for any existing memories we have about road technology research topics? I'd like to see what's already been documented so we can build on our current knowledge base.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"limit": 10, "query": "road technology transportation research pavement materials smart highways electric vehicle infrastructure"}

3. Electric vehicle infrastructure integration - charging networks, inductive charging, grid integration for transportation

messages.content:

**Existing Memory (ID: m2)** - This is the research preferences we just set up:
- Core focus areas: sustainable pavement materials, smart highway technologies, and EV infrastructure integration
- Context: policy initiatives in transportation and road infrastructure

That's the only documentation in your knowledge base so far. It's a solid foundation to build on! As you explore these topics and perhaps uncover more specific areas of interest, we can add those to your research tracking. Would you like to start exploring any of these three core areas in more detail?

Thank you for pulling that up. So we have just the one memory documented so far - the research preferences we just set up with our three core focus areas. That's helpful to see the current state of our knowledge base. Could you give me a bit more detail about what's stored in that memory, or is that essentially the complete picture of what we have documented at this point?

That's essentially the complete picture at this point. The single memory we have (ID: m2) contains:

**Core Focus Areas:**
1. **Sustainable pavement materials** - eco-friendly and recycled materials for road construction and maintenance
2. **Smart highway technologies** - intelligent transportation systems, sensors, and connected vehicle infrastructure
3. **Electric vehicle infrastructure integration** - charging networks, inductive charging, and grid integration for transportation

**Context:** Working on various policy initiatives in transportation and road infrastructure.

That's everything currently documented. It's a concise but solid foundation covering your three main interest areas. As you dive deeper into specific topics within these areas or explore new angles, we can add more detailed memories to build out your knowledge base. Let me know how you'd like to proceed!
